date.headline: Funding awarded to NACDD
date.text: CDC awarded funding to the National Association of Chronic Disease Directors (NACDD) in September 2021 to develop a clinic-to-community framework for arthritis care that enhances healthcare provider awareness, knowledge, and skills in promoting self-management interventions, including physical activity and lifestyle change, as effective, drug-free ways to relieve or manage arthritis pain, improve function, and limit arthritis progression.

date.headline: Landscape Assessment
date.text: Leavitt Partners completed a comprehensive environmental scan and landscape assessment.

date.headline: Medscape CPA
date.text: Medscape learning activity entitled "Lifestyle Management Programs for Arthritis: Expand Your Knowledge on Evidence-Based Interventions" for healthcare professionals.  The goal of the program was for learners to be better able to assess their learning needs related to non-pharmacologic interventions for patients with arthritis and to be able to incorporate arthritis appropriate evidence-based interventions (AAEBIs) into treatment programs.

date.headline: Arthritis Expert Panel 1.0
date.text: Panel engaged in human-centered design sessions to build the framework for the care model.

date.headline: Framework pilot
date.text: Iowa Community HUB contracted to pilot the framework in partnership with Primary Health Care of Des Moines Iowa.

date.headline: Arthritis Expert Panel 2.0
date.text: Advisory group to the pilot project implementation process.

date.headline: Arthritis Expert Panel 3.0
date.text: Advisory group for the development of a strategic plan for a learning health collaborative for scale and spread of the project outputs.

timeline.headline: Development of the Arthritis Evidence-Informed Approach Project
timeline.text:
Advancing Arthritis Public Health Approaches through National Organizations (CDC DP21-2106)
Component 2: Develop and implement an evidence-informed healthcare provider approach to conduct function, pain and physical activity assessments, patient counseling on the benefits of physical activity, and referrals to arthritis appropriate physical activity and self-management programs.
